A courtesy copy of the proof of service must be received by the assigned Examiner by 4/26/21. (The disbursement on 10/4/18 was mischaracterized as conservator fees; it was for trustee fees.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FRANZ VON UCKERMANN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/5/21. (No earlier date available.) STILL no courtesy copy of the supplement filed on 1/13/21 has been provided as of 3/19/21, despite this having been in the tentative ruling for the 2/16/21 hearing. The Declaration filed on 3/9/21 is incomplete. As previously set forth in the hearing notes for the hearing on 8/31/20 and in the tentative ruling for 2/16/21, the person who retains the original must file a declaration certifying that the original document(s) will be retained in the same way as documents are retained under Local Rule 2.11 M.4., which provides that the original(s) must be retained "for at least the period after the termination, including all appeals, of the case required for conventionally filed documents." Furthermore, the declaration signed by Robert Noelke and filed on 3/9/21 is incompletely verified (or seems to indicate that he is not in California), and none of the Corrected I&As filed on 10/6/20 was signed by the other co-executor or properly verified (dated) by Robert Noelke. (Item 4 of an I&A should NOT be completed when a Probate Referee HAS BEEN appointed.) Not all receipts (in particular, refunds) or disbursement are adequately described. The detailed schedules supporting the summary of account must show the nature or purpose of each receipt or disbursement, the source of the receipt or name of the payee, and the date thereof. [Prob. C. Sect. 1062.] A description of what was filed with the Court must be part of the description of disbursements rather than generic "Court Filing Fee", particularly when nothing was filed on the date of the disbursement. There were legal fees paid to Sharma Law Group, but no order authorizing such fees. The accounting schedules show the ending date of the accounting period as 2/28/19. Total cash on hand differs from the one page of the bank statement ending 1/31/19 attached to the petition, but no reconciliation is shown. If courtesy copies of the supplement filed on 1/13/21, the corrected declaration regarding retention of original statements, and any other papers filed to address these issues (including correctly executed and verified Corrected I&As) are not received by the assigned Examiner before 4/23/21, an appearance will be required on 5/5/21.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF THELMA M. THOMPSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/13/2021. 1) No courtesy copy has been received. 2) No proposed order has been received. 3) The proof of service of notice shows an incorrect zip code for Keisha Slocum Smith. 4) Has the petitioner given notice to any creditors and public entities under Probate Code sections 9201 and 9202? 5) Has the petitioner taken any IAEA actions reportable under CRC 7.250(a)? 6) Why is 100% ownership of the Naple Street property still reported as an asset of the surviving spouse's conservatorship estate?

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JERRY PADUA VALMOJA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/20/2021. 1) Must verify cash balances. [LRSF 14.35 D.] 2) Please explain why the caption requests extraordinary fees when no extraordinary fees are requested in the petition. 3) Please address how the creditor claims timeframe has been impacted by the pandemic court orders and emergency rule 9 of the California Rules of Court. 4) On Exhibit 6 please explain entry Gas for $150.00. Long distance travel is reimbursed only in the court discretion, depending on the circumstances disclosed. [LRSF 14.58 C.] 5) On Exhibit 6 please provide legal authority and explain why the estate should incur the cost of 2 rooms for 4 people at the Holiday Inn for $296.48. 6) On Exhibit 6 please explain and file a receipt for Luis Lopez - Hauling of furniture for $4,000.00. [LRSF 14.58 D.] 7) On the Seller's Statement $75,000.00 was paid in commissions and $100,000.00 was held as a security deposit. On Schedule C - Disbursements $75,000.00 is listed as commissions and $75,000.00 is listed as 8/14/2019 Chicago Title Company - Escrow. Please explain as $25k appears unaccounted for. 8) The caption of the order must begin with Order for Final Distribution. [LRSF 14.36 I. 6) b.] An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 4/5/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE JAMES C. GRIMES REVOCABLE INTERVIVOS TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Approval Of Legal Fees And Costs Advanced To Attorneys For Michael S. Torres, The Petitioner For The Appointment Of Bennett Hong As Successor Trustee For The James C. Grimes Revocable Intervivos Trust Dated July 22, 2004, As Amended And Restated In Full Dated December 23, 2015, Granted By This Court, And Established For The Benefit Of Settlor, Former Trustee And Conservatee, James C. Grimes
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/5/21. (No earlier date available.) All petitions involving a trust must state the names and last known addresses of all vested or contingent beneficiaries. [CRC 7.902.] It is unclear what is meant by "an administrative mistake" at the end of paragraph 2 (top of p. 3 of the petition). Some of the time entries appear to be duplicates of entries in the fee petition filed in the conservatorship case: 3/7/19 and 8/5/19 (by PR) and 12/21/18 (by RCN). The filing fee for this petition was $450. The temporary conservator of the person was appointed on 2/7/19, not 8/28/18, and the general conservators were appointed on 4/11/19, not 10/18/18. Courtesy copies any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 4/23/21.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FRANCES K. ROGERS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/13/2021. Issues: 1) The petition for final distribution must state the date on which notice to the Franchise Tax Board was mailed pursuant to Probate Code 9202(c). [LRSF 14.36 I. 2)] 2) The proposed order cannot have an exhibit attached. Please provide an updated proposed order to the examiner without an exhibit. The order must state specifically within the body of the order how the estate is to be distributed, as set forth in the petition. [LRSF 14.36 I. 6) b.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHAEL AQUINO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/13/2021. 1) Objections filed no required courtesy copy provided to Examiner. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. 2) It appears a Will was attempted to be lodged prior to objections being filed and was rejected. 3) Continued to allow Petitioner to file a response to the objections. 4) Petitioner has not filed a response to the Examiner's Notes: a. Petitioner did not select a box on page 1 under 1 and did not specify the name of the newspaper. b. On page 1 #2 under name of Petitioner is blank. c. Please file a copy of Decedent's death certificate. d. On page 1 under #2 c. petitioner did not check a box full or limited authority. e. On page 1 under #2 d. Petitioner selected bond not be required for the reasons stated in item 3e and then under 3e. petitioner did not select a box. f. On page 1 under #3.a Petitioner left place of death blank. g. On page 2 under 3. D. Petitioner states the personal property is $2,700,000.00 and it appears Petitioner alleges the annual gross income from real property is $6,000.00. Please explain why the subtotal adds up to $2,707,200.00. Please explain why gross fair market value of real property is blank if there is $6,000.00 in annual gross income from real property. Please explain why net value of real property is blank. h. Petitioner selected Probate of Lost Will and for Letters Testamentary and then selected on page 2 under #3 f. Decedent died intestate. Please explain. i. On page 2 under #3h petitioner did not select boxes 1 resident of California or 2 nonresident of California and 3 resident of United States or 4 nonresident of United States have not been selected. j. Please explain why Petitioner did not select a box on page 3 under #5 a (1) spouse or (2) no spouse) when there is a spouse listed on page 4 under #8. k. Please explain why Petitioner did not include an Attachment 3(f)(3) that includes a statement of the testamentary words or their substance and states reasons in that attachment why the presumption in Probate Code 6124 does not apply. l. Petitioner has not filed Notice of Petition to Administer Estate (DE-121). m. Petitioner has not filed Duties and Liabilities of Personal Representative (DE-147). n. Petitioner has not filed Proof of Publication. o. A hard copy of the proposed order must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. (LRSF 14.93). [Proposed orders may not be e-filed.]

rulings.Case Title: **WILL TRANSFERRED TO SAN MATEO COUNTY** THE ESTATE OF JOSEFINA G. DIZON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/11/21. Based on the 'Response' filed on 2/1/21 (although no courtesy copy has been provided yet, as of 3/17/21 [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and C.]), Counsel may wish to open the estate in the proper venue and then file an ex parte petition here to request transfer of the original will to the proper court. [Probate Code Section 8203.] Counsel may also wish to review LRSF 14.11 B. regarding procedures for ex parte petitions not requiring an appearance. If nothing further has been done to move this case forward before 4/29/21, an appearance will be required on 5/11/21 for the Court to determine if the petition should be taken off calendar.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANTON BAJJALIEH
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/21/2021 ISSUES: 1) A proposed nonresident personal representative will be required to post a bond to protect California creditors, even if the will waives, or all beneficiaries waive, bond. A declaration or attachment to the petition setting forth in detail the anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate will be used by the Court to determine the amount of the bond, but in no event will the bond be less than $20,000. [LRSF 14.16 F.] 2) Proof of Publication does not indicate Lost Will and must be re-done. Petitions for probate of lost wills must clearly state on their face that the will is lost and both the published and mailed notice must so state. [LRSF 14.20] 3) Notice DE-121 does not indicate Lost Will and must be re-done. Petitions for probate of lost wills must clearly state on their face that the will is lost and both the published and mailed notice must so state. [LRSF 14.20] 4) The addresses on DE-121 for Terah Bajjalieh and Abraham Bajjalieh do not match the addresses on attachment 8 of the petition please explain.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF IVY STEVERSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/13/2021. Issues: 1) Petitioner did not indicate name or date of death of spouse on page 4 under #8. 2) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form. [LRSF 14.18]. 3) Petitioner must give notice of Petition to Administer Estate on judicial council form DE-121. 4) Petiitoner must file Proof of Publication.
